what_it_does: Records metadata for a design-source image asset named recent-pitches/p-26 and labels it as source design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter describes an image blob with id, address, channel, path, blob URI, mime type, zero width/height, source, author session, timestamp, and empty links.
Body text states 'SOURCE design ground-truth — recent-pitches/p-26.jpg'.
No code symbols are present; symbols list is empty.
standouts: Explicit 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label in body.
Width and height are both zero.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of asset metadata fields.
References assets via image://design-source/... addresses and blob:// URIs.
Includes created timestamp and author session.
concerns: Width and height of 0 may indicate missing or uncomputed image dimensions.
Empty links array means no relationships are recorded for this asset.
No alt text, caption, or content description beyond filename.
notes: This is a ledger entry that registers a single JPEG design-source image as ground-truth material with minimal metadata.
questions: What does the empty links array imply about related assets?
Is the blob URI resolvable and does it match the referenced JPEG?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as a design ground-truth source record; the file does exactly that by listing asset metadata and a source label.
intention_signals: To create a discoverable, addressable ledger record for a pitch image so downstream systems can reference it as authoritative design source material.
contribution: Provides a stable addressable identity and blob reference for a design-source image, plus an explicit ground-truth label, which a fused system can use to locate and classify the asset.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata record for the JPEG image recent-pitches/p-26 in the design-source channel, marked as source design ground-truth with blob reference and empty links.
intention_for_embedding: To register and identify a pitch image as authoritative design source material within the channel memory ledger.
